name: Wong Sun v. United States
summary: Fruit of poisonous tree doctrine applies to verbal statements and confessions obtained as result of illegal arrest; attenuation doctrine may allow admission if connection sufficiently attenuated.
holding: Evidence obtained as fruit of unlawful arrest inadmissible unless connection attenuated by intervening circumstances; mere act of later coming to police station voluntarily does not purge taint of illegal arrest if not sufficiently attenuated.
relevance_to_audit: Establishes attenuation analysis for derivative evidence; relevant to determining when tainted evidence chain is sufficiently broken by independent acts.
